title: Ain't Them Bodies Saints
logline: An escaped convict attempts to reunite with his wife and daughter while evading capture, as she struggles between her past love and a new relationship with the lawman who shot him.
antagonistOrConflict: The law/pursuit and the impossibility of reclaiming the past

actBreaks.description: Muldoon finishes his prison workshop shift and the story transitions to Ruth's present life, establishing the parallel worlds they now inhabit.

validation.ruling: Scene #27 shows Ruth picking up her daughter Sylvie from daycare, revealing the major time jump and her new life as a mother, fundamentally shifting the story from the criminal past to the domestic present and establishing the new stakes.

actBreaks.description: Ruth and Smalls have their intimate conversation about Muldoon and their future, representing Ruth's final emotional choice before the climactic confrontation.

validation.ruling: Scene #144 shows Muldoon discovering a threatening figure waiting for him in the farmhouse, creating the final point of no return that launches the climactic sequence and forces the ultimate confrontation.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This script operates as a poetic meditation on love and loss, structured around the gravitational pull between two separated lovers. The first act establishes the tragic separation through the botched robbery and Muldoon's imprisonment, while building Ruth's new life with their daughter and the pursuing lawman Smalls. The second act becomes a tense dance of approach and avoidance as Muldoon escapes and slowly works his way back toward Ruth, while she struggles between past and present. The structure mirrors the characters' emotional states - fragmented, episodic scenes that feel like memories or dreams, building inexorably toward an inevitable tragic reunion.

Lowery uses a circular structure within the linear progression, opening and closing with intimate moments between the lovers in a truck, suggesting the cyclical nature of their doomed romance. The script's episodic nature, with 165 scenes across 89 pages, creates a breathless, almost impressionistic quality that serves the mythic, folkloric tone.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing shifts dramatically between contemplative character moments and bursts of violence. The prison sequence (scenes 14-27) uses montage-like brevity to compress time, while the middle section slows to focus on domestic life and the cat-and-mouse pursuit. The final act accelerates rapidly from scene 145 onward, with the farmhouse shootout and highway chase creating urgent momentum toward the circular ending.

incitingIncident.description: Inside a farmhouse under gunfire, Freddy is shot twice in the chest and collapses while Bob rushes to him and Ruth fires back from a window.

validation.ruling: Scene #1 is setup/ordinary world showing the criminals before their heist. Scene #3 is the true inciting incident where Freddy is shot twice in the chest during the farmhouse robbery gone wrong, shattering their ordinary world and forcing them into irreversible consequences that drive the entire story.

midpoint.description: Muldoon flings himself out the second-story window and lands hard on the ground, quickly moving into the shadows.

validation.ruling: Scene #80 is just Muldoon escaping from a window. Scene #71 at the farmhouse is the true midpoint where Muldoon confronts his past and makes the pivotal decision to return to his criminal life, shifting from reactive hiding to proactive action and raising the stakes significantly.

climax.description: Muldoon tumbles down a hill into a stream, tackles an armed pursuer, and struggles for control of the gun before multiple shots fire into the darkness.

validation.ruling: Scene #148 shows Muldoon driving away after the confrontation, which is aftermath rather than climax. Scene #146 is the true climax where Muldoon tumbles into a stream, tackles an armed pursuer, and struggles for control of the gun with multiple shots fired - this is the peak physical confrontation and highest dramatic tension.

scenes.heading: 1 INT. TRUCK - NIGHT 1 1
scenes.summary: Bob Muldoon and Ruth sit in a truck at night with a six-shooter beside them, waiting for their accomplice Freddy who emerges from the darkness near a brick wall.

scenes.heading: 3 EXT. COUNTRY - DAY 3 3
scenes.summary: Gunshots ring out in the distance across the Texas hill country.

scenes.heading: 4 INT. FARMHOUSE - THAT MOMENT 4 4
scenes.summary: Inside a farmhouse under gunfire, Freddy is shot twice in the chest and collapses while Bob rushes to him and Ruth fires back from a window.
scenes.turningPointNote: The robbery goes catastrophically wrong - Freddy is fatally wounded and they're trapped

scenes.heading: 5 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 5 5
scenes.summary: The gunfire stops and smoke clears, revealing the farmhouse surrounded by a phalanx of police officers.

scenes.heading: 6 INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 6 6
scenes.summary: Ruth tells Bob she shot someone while he holds the dying Freddy in his bloodied hands.

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. FARMHOUSE - THAT MOMENT 7 7
scenes.summary: Police gather around a wounded young officer who was shot, calling for an ambulance over the radio.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. FARMHOUSE - THE NEXT DAY 8 8
scenes.summary: Freddy dies in Bob's arms while Ruth watches, then Bob embraces Ruth, getting blood on her from his hands.

scenes.heading: 9 EXT. FARMHOUSE - THAT MOMENT 9 9
scenes.summary: Police slowly advance through the grass toward the farmhouse with their guns drawn.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. FARMHOUSE - THAT MOMENT 10 10
scenes.summary: Ruth shakes her head tearfully inside the farmhouse.

scenes.heading: 11 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 11 11
scenes.summary: Police take positions around the house, forming a perimeter and flanking the porch.

scenes.heading: 12 INT. FARMHOUSE - THAT MOMENT 12 12
scenes.summary: Bob asks Ruth to wait for him and she nods as police shadows move across the walls.

scenes.heading: 13 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 13 13
scenes.summary: Bob and Ruth surrender, emerging with hands up, but Bob steals a final kiss before being forced to his knees by police.
scenes.turningPointNote: Their capture separates the lovers, setting up the central conflict of the story

scenes.heading: 14 INT. PRISON YARD - MORNING 14 14
scenes.summary: An empty prison exercise yard surrounded by red brick walls is shown.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. PRISON CELL - DAY 15 15
scenes.summary: A prison guard tells Bob through his cell bars that he has a baby girl, and Bob retreats into his cell smiling.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bob learns he's become a father while imprisoned, deepening his motivation to return

scenes.heading: 18 INT. PRISON CELL - MORNING 18 18
scenes.summary: Bob writes a letter in his cell under a skylight, with a photo of Ruth and their baby daughter pinned to the wall.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. PRISON BLOCK - MORNING 19 19
scenes.summary: A guard's baton raps against cell bars as prisoners begin to stir for the day.

scenes.heading: 20 INT. PRISON WORKSHOP - DAY 20 20
scenes.summary: Bob works at a jigsaw in the prison workshop, cutting wood pieces for chair assembly.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. PRISON YARD - AFTERNOON 21 21
scenes.summary: Bob runs laps around the prison yard, having paid a guard for extra time to run fourteen miles.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. PRISON MESS HALL - DAY 22 22
scenes.summary: In the prison mess hall, Bob is suddenly punched by a small prisoner in a wifebeater.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. SOLITARY CELL - DAY 23 23
scenes.summary: Bruised and bloodied Bob sits in a solitary confinement cell as the door shuts, leaving him in darkness.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. SOLITARY CELL - DAY 24 24
scenes.summary: The solitary cell door opens, revealing a newly bearded Bob squinting at the light.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. PRISON SHOWERS - DAY 25 25
scenes.summary: Bob washes weeks of grime and dried blood from his skin in the prison showers.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. CELL - DAY 26 26
scenes.summary: Bob returns to his regular cell and finishes writing his letter, signing it with love.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. PRISON WORKSHOP - DAY 27 27
scenes.summary: Bob works at his jigsaw in the prison workshop, then leaves when the whistle blows as the camera focuses on his saw blade.

scenes.heading: 28 EXT. NURSERY - AFTERNOON 28 28
scenes.summary: Ruth, now 27 and calmer, plants a sapling at a garden nursery where she works.

scenes.heading: 29 INT. NURSERY - AFTERNOON 29 29
scenes.summary: Ruth rings up a customer at the nursery register and glances at the clock as daylight wanes.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. DAYCARE - AFTERNOON 30 30
scenes.summary: Ruth picks up her four-year-old daughter Sylvie from daycare, lifting her into her arms.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. RUTH’S CAR - NIGHT 31 31
scenes.summary: Ruth drives while Sylvie tells her a story about a man who went hunting and was eaten by a bear.

scenes.heading: 32 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - AFTERNOON 32 32
scenes.summary: Sylvie plays with a litter of motherless kittens at their kitchen table while Ruth watches.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. BEDROOM - RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 33 33
scenes.summary: Ruth reads a bedtime story to Sylvie in bed, pointing at pictures of a crying star.

scenes.heading: 34 INT. BEDROOM - RUTH’S HOUSE - MORNING 34 34
scenes.summary: Sylvie tries to wake her sleeping mother by gently touching her head and pushing her shoulder.

scenes.heading: 35 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - MORNING 35 35
scenes.summary: Deputy Patrick Smalls, the officer who was shot at the farmhouse, sits in a police car outside Ruth's house then gets out.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 36 36
scenes.summary: Smalls visits Ruth at breakfast, joking with Sylvie about the kittens before Ruth sends Sylvie away and Smalls warns Ruth that Bob has escaped prison.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bob's escape is revealed, putting Ruth in immediate danger and restarting the central pursuit

scenes.heading: 37 INT. RUTH’S CAR - DAY 37 37
scenes.summary: Ruth drives while crying as Sylvie watches her mother with concern from the backseat.

scenes.heading: 38 EXT. WOODS - DAY 38 38
scenes.summary: Bob emerges from the woods covered in mud, waits for traffic to clear, then crosses the highway.

scenes.heading: 39 EXT. ROADSIDE DINER - DAY 39 39
scenes.summary: Bob approaches a woman named Sissy at a roadside diner, grabs her arm, and shoves her into her car as she screams.

scenes.heading: 40 INT. WOMAN’S CAR - DAY 40 40
scenes.summary: Bob forces the terrified woman to drive while she recognizes him and asks what he's going to do to her.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - AFTERNOON 41 41
scenes.summary: Ruth is questioned at the police station about Bob's escape attempts and his letters, remaining stone-faced and composed.

scenes.heading: 42 EXT. TRAIN STATION - DAY 42 42
scenes.summary: Police and dogs swarm a halted train at a rural station searching for Bob.

scenes.heading: 43 EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY 43 43
scenes.summary: An abandoned car lies in a ditch where prison clothes were found ten miles up the road.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 44 44
scenes.summary: Ruth's face is held in close-up as the detective explains Bob appears to be heading north.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. PRINTING PRESS - CONTINUOUS 45 45
scenes.summary: A printing press rolls off wanted posters displaying Bob's photograph.

scenes.heading: 46 EXT. FARM - DAY 46 46
scenes.summary: Police dogs and officers search through brush on an overgrown farm.

scenes.heading: 47 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 47 47
scenes.summary: Ruth casts her eyes down and responds briefly to the detective's questioning.

scenes.heading: 48 EXT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - CONTINUOUS 48 48
scenes.summary: Ruth rushes from the police station to her car as Smalls follows and catches a glimpse of her face as she drives away.

scenes.heading: 49 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - DAY 49 49
scenes.summary: Smalls returns to find Cowboy Hat waiting, who orders surveillance on Ruth's house and assigns Smalls to catch Bob.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 50 50
scenes.summary: Ruth drives to the old farmhouse where police are swarming, but an officer stops her from going inside.

scenes.heading: 51 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - EVENING 51 51
scenes.summary: Ruth returns home with Sylvie, notices the police surveillance car, and is approached by her elderly neighbor Skerritt.

scenes.heading: 52 EXT. SMALL TOWN - EVENING 52 52
scenes.summary: Bob slides down an overpass and heads toward a small industrial town with a steel mill.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. JOANIE’S HOUSE - EVENING 53 53
scenes.summary: Bob knocks on the back door of a tenement home and asks an old woman named Joanie for something Jake told him she would give him.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. JOANIE’S BATHROOM - JOANIE’S HOUSE - EVENING 54 54
scenes.summary: Muldoon showers and trims his beard and mustache in Joanie's bathroom, refining his appearance.

scenes.heading: 55 INT. JOANIE’S BEDROOM - JOANIE’S HOUSE - EVENING 55 55
scenes.summary: Joanie gives Muldoon work boots and returns a tattered photograph of Ruth and Sylvie that was in his pocket.

scenes.heading: 56 INT. JOANIE’S KITCHEN - EVENING 56 56
scenes.summary: Muldoon eats hurriedly while Joanie watches him like a mother, and he asks for a map.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. CHURCH - MORNING 57 57
scenes.summary: Ruth and Sylvie walk to church on Sunday morning, and Ruth notices a police car parked down the street.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. CHURCH - DAY 58 58
scenes.summary: During church service, Ruth sits in the back pew with her lips shut while people glance and whisper, and Smalls looks back at her from the front.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. MAUDE’S - NIGHT 59 59
scenes.summary: The scene establishes Maude's bar in the hills with music, smoke, customers, and Sweetie behind the bar.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. MAUDE’S - NIGHT 60 60
scenes.summary: After closing, Sweetie tells Muldoon about buying the bar and his five-year plan while Muldoon pets the dog and asks for a dime.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. MAUDE’S - NIGHT 61 61
scenes.summary: Sweetie shows Muldoon an old truck behind the bar, kicks over the woodblocks, and gives him the keys as the engine starts.

scenes.heading: 62 INT. MAUDE’S - UPSTAIRS - LATER 62 62
scenes.summary: Sweetie sleeps on the bed while Muldoon lies on a pallet on the floor, listening to the wind in the trees.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. MAUDE’S - NIGHT 63 63
scenes.summary: Muldoon finds a shotgun with a single shell hidden under the bar counter in the darkened tavern.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. BEDROOM - RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 64 64
scenes.summary: Ruth lies awake in bed, goes to the window, and sees a police car parked across the street.

scenes.heading: 65 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER 65 65
scenes.summary: Ruth approaches the police car where Smalls is waiting and invites him inside her house.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 66 66
scenes.summary: Ruth gives Smalls a shoebox full of all the letters Muldoon sent her, along with photos and a silver ring necklace he gave her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruth surrenders all of Muldoon's letters and mementos to the police, symbolically severing their connection

scenes.heading: 67 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - DAWN 67 67
scenes.summary: An old truck drives down a dirt road through rolling meadows at dawn.

scenes.heading: 68 EXT. WOODS - DAWN 68 68
scenes.summary: Muldoon waits in the woods for a whistle signal, then emerges with an old shovel to meet Sweetie at the farmlands.

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 69 69
scenes.summary: Muldoon and Sweetie approach the old farmhouse as Sweetie mentions kids come to see where it all went down and think it's haunted.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 70 70
scenes.summary: Inside the ruined farmhouse, Muldoon finds initials carved in a heavy wooden table and heaves it upright, shaking the whole house.

scenes.heading: 71 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 71 71
scenes.summary: Muldoon digs at the ruins of an old stone cottage and unearths a buried metal box from the soil.
scenes.turningPointNote: Muldoon retrieves what appears to be hidden money or valuables, giving him resources for his plan

scenes.heading: 72 EXT. DOWNTOWN - DAY 72 72
scenes.summary: The scene establishes downtown Main Street and an alley behind Skerritt's Old Home Supply store.

scenes.heading: 73 EXT. ALLEY - CONTINUOUS 73 73
scenes.summary: Muldoon parks in the alley and casually walks into Skerritt's store.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. SKERRITT’S - CONTINUOUS 74 74
scenes.summary: In Skerritt's antique shop, Muldoon and Skerritt discuss his escape and Muldoon asks about buying a gun, with Skerritt questioning his wisdom in returning to his hometown.

scenes.heading: 75 EXT. SKERRITT’S - DAY 75 75
scenes.summary: Skerritt watches Muldoon's truck disappear from the loading dock behind his shop.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. MULDOON’S TRUCK - DAY 76 76
scenes.summary: Muldoon drives agitated while talking about someone giving Ruth a house, and they pass a police car on the road.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - DAY 77 77
scenes.summary: Smalls enters the police station carrying Ruth's box of letters and takes candy from an advent calendar.

scenes.heading: 78 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - LATER 78 78
scenes.summary: Smalls spreads out photographs and newspaper clippings at his desk, studying the criminal history and seeing photos of a young teenage Muldoon.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. MAUDE’S - UPSTAIRS - NIGHT 79 79
scenes.summary: Muldoon sits at a table with a map showing a northward route and begins writing a letter to Ruth.

scenes.heading: 80 EXT. MAUDE’S - CONTINUOUS 80 80
scenes.summary: Three police cars pull up to Maude's tavern.

scenes.heading: 81 INT. MAUDE’S - DOWNSTAIRS - CONTINUOUS 81 81
scenes.summary: Smalls and two cops enter the bar looking for Muldoon, and Sweetie denies seeing him while allowing them to search.

scenes.heading: 82 INT. MAUDE’S - UPSTAIRS - CONTINUOUS 82 82
scenes.summary: Muldoon grabs books and his belongings, knocking the photo of Sylvie to the floor as he hears footsteps on the stairs and opens the window.

scenes.heading: 83 EXT. MAUDE’S - CONTINUOUS 83 83
scenes.summary: Muldoon flings himself out the second-story window and lands hard on the ground, quickly moving into the shadows.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. MAUDE’S - UPSTAIRS - CONTINUOUS 84 84
scenes.summary: Sweetie shows the cops around his apartment while Smalls notices the photo of Sylvie on the floor.

scenes.heading: 85 EXT. MAUDE’S - CONTINUOUS 85 85
scenes.summary: Muldoon darts between trees away from the bar, watching the police cars out front.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. MAUDE’S - UPSTAIRS - CONTINUOUS 86 86
scenes.summary: Smalls questions Sweetie about the photo of Sylvie, and Sweetie claims Ruth mailed it to send to Bob but he never did.

scenes.heading: 87 EXT. MAUDE’S - CONTINUOUS 87 87
scenes.summary: Muldoon hides in the trees as the police shine flashlights and leave, then emerges in the moonlight after their taillights disappear.

scenes.heading: 88 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - NIGHT 88 88
scenes.summary: At the police station, Smalls tells another cop they found nothing and places the photo of Sylvie in his desk drawer.

scenes.heading: 89 EXT. WOODS - DAY 89 89
scenes.summary: Muldoon's truck sits parked and half-hidden in the woods.

scenes.heading: 90 INT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 90 90
scenes.summary: Muldoon climbs into the rafters of the farmhouse and curls up with a blanket to keep watch as thunder rumbles.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. FARMHOUSE - LATER 91 91
scenes.summary: Muldoon awakens as rain pours through holes in the roof, soaking him in his corner.

scenes.heading: 92 EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 92 92
scenes.summary: Muldoon runs through the rain to a shed outside the farmhouse and rummages for tools.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 93 93
scenes.summary: Muldoon attempts to patch the biggest hole in the ceiling with plywood and nails.

scenes.heading: 94 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 94 94
scenes.summary: The sound of Muldoon's hammering echoes across the hills.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. SKERRITT’S - DAY 95 95
scenes.summary: At Skerritt's shop, three men enter including one who resembles a bear, and they split up to browse while Skerritt watches.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. SKERRITT’S - LATER 96 96
scenes.summary: Someone enters Skerritt's shop and places a folded note on his desk, which he reads after they leave.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. MAUDE’S - AFTERNOON 97 97
scenes.summary: Skerritt enters Maude's bar and tells Sweetie he has a message to deliver, asking for help as a courier.

scenes.heading: 98 EXT. FARMHOUSE - AFTERNOON 98 98
scenes.summary: At sunset, Sweetie approaches the farmhouse and gives Muldoon a note, which Muldoon reads before looking back at the farmhouse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Muldoon receives a message that appears to be a warning or threat, escalating the danger

scenes.heading: 99 EXT. NURSERY - AFTERNOON 99 99
scenes.summary: Ruth lifts flowers from the ground and places them in wooden pots at the nursery on a blustery winter day.

scenes.heading: 100 INT. POLICE STATION - DAY 100 100
scenes.summary: Smalls tells a colleague he's going to lunch and leaves the police station.

scenes.heading: 101 EXT. POLICE STATION - CONTINUOUS 101 101
scenes.summary: Smalls walks out to his car from the police station.

scenes.heading: 102 EXT. NURSERY - AFTERNOON 102 102
scenes.summary: At the nursery, Mary asks Ruth if she needs time off because of the news coverage, but Ruth deflects the question.

scenes.heading: 103 INT. RUTH’S CAR - AFTERNOON 103 103
scenes.summary: Ruth drives hurriedly out of downtown, letting her hair down as her car engine strains.

scenes.heading: 104 INT. PRESCHOOL - AFTERNOON 104 104
scenes.summary: Ruth arrives late to pick up Sylvie from preschool, and Sylvie destroys her Lincoln Log house when told they have to leave.

scenes.heading: 105 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - AFTERNOON 105 105
scenes.summary: Smalls drives his police car down a narrow dirt road, creating a cloud of dust.

scenes.heading: 106 INT. CAR - AFTERNOON 106 106
scenes.summary: Ruth buckles Sylvie into her car seat and tells her their plans for errands and asks what she wants for dinner.

scenes.heading: 107 INT. POST OFFICE- AFTERNOON 107 107
scenes.summary: Ruth picks up a missed delivery at the post office and notices Muldoon's wanted poster on the bulletin board.

scenes.heading: 108 EXT. FARMHOUSE - AFTERNOON 108 108
scenes.summary: Smalls parks at the farmhouse, slams his car door loudly to announce his presence, and notices fresh tire treads in the dirt.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 109 109
scenes.summary: Smalls enters the empty farmhouse with his gun drawn, finding smoke from the wood-burning stove and searching the rooms.

scenes.heading: 110 EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER 110 110
scenes.summary: Smalls leaves the farmhouse and heads back to his car.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. SIDE OF THE ROAD - AFTERNOON 111 111
scenes.summary: Ruth's car breaks down on the side of the road with steam coming from the engine, and she carries Sylvie toward a nearby gas station.

scenes.heading: 112 INT. GAS STATION - AFTERNOON 112 112
scenes.summary: Ruth uses the phone at a gas station while the patrons recognize her and murmur among themselves.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. POLICE CAR - AFTERNOON 113 113
scenes.summary: Smalls drives back toward town when his radio crackles.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. POLICE CAR - LATER 114 114
scenes.summary: Ruth and Sylvie sit in Smalls' police car as he lets Sylvie turn on the sirens.

scenes.heading: 115 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - AFTERNOON 115 115
scenes.summary: Ruth, Sylvie, and Smalls enter Ruth's house with groceries, and Ruth goes to change clothes.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - DAY 116 116
scenes.summary: Ruth sits in her room writing a letter that begins "Dear Bob" while kittens play at her feet.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. BEDROOM - RUTH’S HOUSE - DAY 117 117
scenes.summary: Ruth goes through old boxes in her closet and shows Sylvie a tiny baptismal dress, telling her it was the first dress she ever wore.

scenes.heading: 118 INT. RUTH’S CAR - DAY 118 118
scenes.summary: Ruth drives with a box of kittens beside her, stops at an indeterminate building, and looks down at the kittens for comfort.

scenes.heading: 119 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - EVENING 119 119
scenes.summary: Ruth crumples up her letter and begins writing another one that starts "Dear Bob."

scenes.heading: 120 EXT. MAIN STREET - DAY 120 120
scenes.summary: Three children run down the street playing with toy guns, one wearing Muldoon's wanted poster as a mask with eye holes.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - DAY 121 121
scenes.summary: Ruth hesitates while writing her letter when Smalls appears in the bedroom doorway asking if she's ready to go.

scenes.heading: 122 EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 122 122
scenes.summary: Muldoon washes his face with well water outside the farmhouse on a winter day.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. CHRISTMAS TREE FARM - DAY 123 123
scenes.summary: Ruth, Sylvie, and Smalls walk through a Christmas tree farm looking for a tree, with Smalls carrying a hacksaw.

scenes.heading: 124 INT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 124 124
scenes.summary: Muldoon retrieves the satchel of money from under the farmhouse floorboards.

scenes.heading: 125 EXT. CHRISTMAS TREE FARM - DAY 125 125
scenes.summary: A sawblade cuts into the trunk of a Christmas tree.

scenes.heading: 126 INT. MULDOON’S TRUCK - DAY 126 126
scenes.summary: Muldoon drives his truck from the woods onto the main road.

scenes.heading: 127 EXT. CHRISTMAS TREE FARM - LATER 127 127
scenes.summary: Ruth loses sight of Sylvie among the Christmas trees and calls out her name while searching frantically.

scenes.heading: 128 INT. MAUDE’S - UPSTAIRS - DAY 128 128
scenes.summary: Muldoon changes into nice clothes and tells Sweetie that Ruth will meet him at his father's place at sunrise.

scenes.heading: 129 EXT. CHRISTMAS TREE FARM - LATER 129 129
scenes.summary: Ruth, Sylvie, and Smalls gather at the Christmas tree farm's base camp where trees are being sold and kids roast marshmallows by a bonfire.

scenes.heading: 130 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 130 130
scenes.summary: Smalls helps Sylvie decorate the Christmas tree while Ruth watches from the kitchen, her smile fading when they're not looking.

scenes.heading: 131 INT. MAUDE’S - BACK ROOM - NIGHT 131 131
scenes.summary: Muldoon examines fake identification documents with the name Warren Miller and asks Sweetie about the dead people whose identities they're using.

scenes.heading: 132 INT. RUTH’S HOME - NIGHT 132 132
scenes.summary: Ruth carries her sleeping daughter to the bedroom.

scenes.heading: 133 EXT. MAUDE’S - NIGHT 133 133
scenes.summary: Muldoon says goodbye to Sweetie outside Maude's, promising no more trouble and mentioning Montana.

scenes.heading: 134 INT. MAUDE’S - CONTINUOUS 134 134
scenes.summary: Sweetie enters the bar and dances while three men in the corner watch him.

scenes.heading: 135 INT. MULDOON’S TRUCK - NIGHT 135 135
scenes.summary: Muldoon drives his truck, stops at a four-way intersection, then reverses and turns left instead of continuing straight.
scenes.turningPointNote: Muldoon changes course, deciding not to go to the planned meeting spot

scenes.heading: 136 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 136 136
scenes.summary: The completed Christmas tree stands in Ruth's house with a makeshift paper star on top.

scenes.heading: 137 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 137 137
scenes.summary: Ruth asks Smalls to stay and embraces him when he agrees.

scenes.heading: 138 EXT. NEIGHBORHOOD - NIGHT 138 138
scenes.summary: Muldoon parks in Ruth's neighborhood and walks to a corner where he can see her house and the police car parked outside.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 139 139
scenes.summary: Ruth kisses Smalls tentatively, but they're interrupted when Sylvie cries out from the bedroom.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 140 140
scenes.summary: Muldoon watches through the window as Ruth, Sylvie, and Smalls sit together on the couch in the warm light, then slips away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Muldoon sees Ruth with Smalls and realizes she has moved on

scenes.heading: 141 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 141 141
scenes.summary: Ruth sits on the couch cradling sleeping Sylvie while Smalls sits beside her.

scenes.heading: 142 EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - NIGHT 142 142
scenes.summary: Muldoon drives his truck on an empty highway, pulls over, gets out and kicks dust while muttering to himself.

scenes.heading: 143 INT. BEDROOM - RUTH’S HOUSE - NIGHT 143 143
scenes.summary: Sylvie sleeps in bed next to a wastebasket filled with Ruth's crumpled, unfinished letters.

scenes.heading: 144 INT. RUTH’S HOME - NIGHT 144 144
scenes.summary: Smalls tells Ruth he has no grudge against Bob and that his feelings for her are genuine, then asks if she misses Bob.

scenes.heading: 145 EXT. WOODS - NIGHT 145 145
scenes.summary: Muldoon walks through the woods after leaving his truck in a hiding spot.

scenes.heading: 146 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 146 146
scenes.summary: Muldoon emerges from the woods and heads up the hill toward the farmhouse.

scenes.heading: 147 INT. FARMHOUSE - NIGHT 147 147
scenes.summary: Muldoon enters the farmhouse and discovers a bearded man in a big coat sitting at the table in the darkness.

scenes.heading: 148 EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 148 148
scenes.summary: Muldoon leaps through the door as gunshots explode behind him, rolls down the porch, and runs into the darkness while more shots follow.

scenes.heading: 149 EXT. WOODS - CONTINUOUS 149 149
scenes.summary: Muldoon tumbles down a hill into a stream, tackles an armed pursuer, and struggles for control of the gun before multiple shots fire into the darkness.

scenes.heading: 150 EXT. WOODS - NIGHT 150 150
scenes.summary: Muldoon's truck emerges from the woods and swerves back onto the road.

scenes.heading: 151 INT. MULDOON’S TRUCK - CONTINUOUS 151 151
scenes.summary: Muldoon drives covered in blood, breathing hard from pain, when his truck engine begins to choke and the speedometer drops despite pressing the gas.

scenes.heading: 152 EXT. HIGHWAY - DAWN 152 152
scenes.summary: Muldoon's truck breaks down on the highway at dawn, steam billowing from the engine, and he gets out with his gun.

scenes.heading: 153 EXT. SERVICE STATION - MORNING 153 153
scenes.summary: Muldoon watches a gas station, waits for the attendant to go make change, then yanks open a car door and slides into the passenger seat while the driver protests.
scenes.turningPointNote: Muldoon resorts to carjacking, escalating his desperation

scenes.heading: 154 EXT. HIGHWAY - CONTINUOUS 154 154
scenes.summary: Wounded Muldoon directs a nervous driver down the highway while fashioning a tourniquet from his jacket and having the driver help tighten it to stop his bleeding.

scenes.heading: 155 INT. BEDROOM - RUTH’S HOUSE - MORNING 155 155
scenes.summary: Sylvie wakes up alone in bed and realizes she's by herself.

scenes.heading: 156 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 156 156
scenes.summary: Sylvie finds Ruth and Smalls together on the couch under a sheet, Ruth smiles reassuringly at her daughter, but a gunshot suddenly shatters the silence.
scenes.turningPointNote: The gunshot marks the violent climax arriving at Ruth's doorstep

scenes.heading: 157 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 157 157
scenes.summary: Smalls shoots Bear in the head after Bear wounds Skerritt, then runs to check on the injured Skerritt on his porch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Bear is killed, ending the immediate threat to Ruth and her family

scenes.heading: 158 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - DAY 158 158
scenes.summary: Police cars and an ambulance surround the houses with Bear's body covered by a sheet while Ruth holds Sylvie in a police car.

scenes.heading: 159 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - DAY 159 159
scenes.summary: Ruth sits with Sylvie at the police station and tearfully tells Smalls she's worried about Bob and knows where he is, asking him to help.

scenes.heading: 160 INT. POLICE DEPARTMENT - MOMENTS LATER 160 160
scenes.summary: Smalls tells Cowboy Hat he's taking Ruth home to pack before she goes to her mother's, and they discuss the identity of the dead man.

scenes.heading: 161 EXT. SMALLS’ CAR - AFTERNOON 161 161
scenes.summary: Smalls drives Ruth and Sylvie in silence as snow begins to fall on the windshield.

scenes.heading: 162 EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 162 162
scenes.summary: Snow falls on the farmhouse as Ruth and Sylvie sit in Smalls' car, then Ruth carries Sylvie toward the farmhouse after Smalls motions to her.

scenes.heading: 163 INT. FARMHOUSE - DAY 163 163
scenes.summary: Ruth carries Sylvie through the cold, empty farmhouse with snow drifting through the hole in the ceiling, finding no sign of life.

scenes.heading: 164 EXT. WOODS - CONTINUOUS 164 164
scenes.summary: Snow falls on the bodies of the two men Muldoon killed in the woods, one by the creek and another who died crawling away.

scenes.heading: 165 INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS 165 165
scenes.summary: Ruth nods to Smalls that she's ready to leave the farmhouse, and they head toward the door with Smalls taking one last look around.

scenes.heading: 166 EXT. RUTH’S HOUSE - EVENING 166 166
scenes.summary: Smalls' squad car returns to Ruth's house in the evening as snow continues to fall.

scenes.heading: 167 INT. RUTH’S HOUSE - EVENING 167 167
scenes.summary: Ruth enters her house with Smalls and Sylvie, discovers a bloodstained letter on the kitchen table addressed to her, while Sylvie walks toward the bedroom.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ruth discovers Bob's bloodstained letter, confirming his presence and likely fate

scenes.heading: 168 INT. CAR - NIGHT 168 168
scenes.summary: In a flashback to summer, young Ruth and Muldoon lie together in a car as he describes his vision of their future together as an old couple waving from their own house.

structuralWarnings.text: All 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
